description_long: This is a two-story wooden bungalow with a gable roof and a prominent front porch supported by square columns and a wooden balustrade. The exterior is clad in horizontal siding painted a light gray, with a stone or brick foundation visible at the base. A single brick chimney rises along one side of the house, adding to its traditional appearance. The front facade features a symmetrical arrangement of two windows on the upper floor and two on the lower, flanking a central entrance that leads onto the porch. The side elevations show additional windows, providing ample natural light to the interior. The overall design is simple and functional, typical of early 20th-century American residential architecture.
description: A two-story, light gray wooden bungalow with a gable roof, front porch, and symmetrical window arrangement. Features include a brick chimney, stone foundation, and classic horizontal siding.
description_view_0: Side view showing the brick chimney, a single window, and the side of the front porch with its balustrade.
description_view_1: Front view highlighting the gable roof, central entrance, full-width porch, and symmetrical window arrangement.
description_view_2: Opposite side view with two pairs of windows and the side of the porch visible, no chimney present.
description_view_3: Rear view displaying a plain gable wall with horizontal siding and no windows or architectural features.
